pi_items.source: File path is consistent with task description and shell scripts. .drawio is the standard draw.io file format.
File path is consistent with task description and export_result.sh. PNG is standard export format for draw.io.
export_result.sh line 117: checks for 'valves' (plural) in style attribute for fluid power shape detection, not 'valve' (singular)
export_result.sh line 117 checks for 'fluid_power' in style. Confirmed via web search: draw.io has Fluid Power shapes (ISO 1219) library (https://x.com/drawio/status/1265901759475650560)
export_result.sh line 117 checks for 'hydraulic' in style. Consistent with ISO 1219 hydraulic standard.
export_result.sh line 131 checks for 'pump' in style. Pump is listed as required component in task description.
export_result.sh line 132 checks for 'cylinder' in style. Double-Acting Cylinder is explicitly required in task description.
export_result.sh line 134 checks for 'reservoir' or 'tank' in style. Reservoir/Tank is first component listed in task description.
export_result.sh line 123 checks for 'pump' in value/label. Explicitly mentioned in task description as required label.
export_result.sh line 124 checks for 'cylinder' in value/label. 'Lift Cylinder' mentioned in task description.
export_result.sh line 125 checks for 'valve' in value/label. 'Relief Valve' mentioned in task description.
export_result.sh line 128 checks for 'filter' in value/label. Return Line Filter is required component per task description.
export_result.sh line 126 checks for 'tank' or 'reservoir' in value/label. Hydraulic Reservoir (Tank) is first component listed.
export_result.sh line 127 checks for 'motor' in value/label. Electric Motor is required component per setup_task.sh specs.

Task description mentions 'ISO 1219 standard symbols'. Web search confirms these are international standards for fluid power graphical symbols. https://www.iso.org/standard/51200.html and https://www.iso.org/standard/60184.html
install_drawio.sh line 67: DRAWIO_VERSION="26.0.9". GitHub release download URL: https://github.com/jgraph/drawio-desktop/releases/download/v26.0.9/drawio-amd64-26.0.9.deb

setup_task.sh lines 10-40: The hydraulic_specs.txt file is created by the setup script using 'cat > /home/ga/Desktop/hydraulic_specs.txt << EOF' syntax. The specifications are not downloaded from a URL; they are embedded in the setup script.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a hydraulic circuit diagram using draw.io 26.0.9 with Fluid Power shapes (ISO 1219 standard). Required file outputs are forklift_hydraulic.drawio and forklift_hydraulic.png saved to Desktop. The task is based on synthetic hydraulic specifications (Series-X Forklift system) generated during setup, including components: Electric Motor, Fixed Displacement Pump, Hydraulic Reservoir, Pressure Relief Valve, 4/3 Directional Control Valve, Double-Acting Cylinder, and Return Line Filter. The metadata contains one discrepancy: 'required_styles' lists 'valve' (singular) but the export verification script checks for 'valves' (plural) when detecting Fluid Power library usage. The data source is synthetic (not real-world).
